articles.headline: How every five-star men's college basketball commit fits at his new school
articles.description: Breaking down top class of 2026 commitments, including a third five-star recruit for BYU in three years.

images.name: Bruce Branch III [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruce Branch III, the former No. 2 prospect who reclassified up from the 2027 class, has committed to BYU, giving Kevin Young a third consecutive class featuring a five-star recruit.

images.name: Jordan Smith [1296x729]
images.caption: Jordan Smith is the second five-star recruit of the 2026 high school class to commit to Arkansas.

images.name: Cameron Williams commit fit
images.caption: A top-3 recruit in the class of 2026, Cameron Williams is headed to Durham to play for Jon Scheyer and the Duke Blue Devils.

images.name: Jason Crowe [1144x644]
images.caption: Jason Crowe, No. 4 recruit in the 2026 ESPN 100 player rankings, has committed to Missouri.

images.name: Babatunde Oladotun [1066x600]
images.caption: Babatunde Oladotun, the former 2027 No. 1 prospect who reclassified to the 2026 class and is ranked in the top-10, has announced his commitment to Maryland.

images.name: Dylan Mingo [1296x729]
images.caption: Premier class of 2026 guard Dylan Mingo has committed to North Carolina for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Caleb Gaskins [1296x729]
images.caption: Caleb Gaskins is staying in-state next season to play for Miami and first-time head coach Jai Lucas.

images.name: Deron Rippey Jr. [1296x729]
images.caption: Deron Rippey Jr. has committed to Duke, giving the Blue Devils another top recruit.

images.name: Toni Bryant (commitment photo) [600x338]
images.caption: Toni Bryant, the No. 14 recruit in the class of 2026, has committed to Missouri, giving the Tigers two five-star prospects for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Austin Goosby (Texas) [1296x729]
images.caption: Austin Goosby has committed to Sean Miller and Texas for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Taylen Kinney [1296x729]
images.caption: Taylen Kinney is one of the best point guards in the boys' SC Next class of 2026, and recently committed to Kansas.
images.credit: Brian Rothmuller/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

article.headline: Long Island Sharks and Chicago State Cougars play in NEC Tournament
article.description: Chicago State Cougars (7-24, 5-13 NEC) at Long Island Sharks (21-10, 15-3 NEC)
article.title: Long Island Sharks and Chicago State Cougars play in NEC Tournament
article.linkText: Long Island Sharks and Chicago State Cougars play in NEC Tournament

categories.description: Chicago State Cougars @ Long Island University Sharks

event.description: Chicago State Cougars @ Long Island University Sharks

keywords: Chicago State Cougars @ Long Island University Sharks
article.story: Chicago State Cougars (7-24, 5-13 NEC) at Long Island Sharks (21-10, 15-3 NEC) New York; Wednesday, 7 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: LIU plays Chicago State in the NEC Tournament. The Sharks' record in NEC games is 15-3, and their record is 6-7 in non-conference play. LIU is third in the NEC in rebounding with 32.3 rebounds. Greg Gordon leads the Sharks with 5.6 boards. The Cougars are 5-13 in NEC play. Chicago State averages 9.6 turnovers per game and is 7-14 when turning the ball over less than opponents. LIU scores 74.1 points per game, 1.9 fewer points than the 76.0 Chicago State gives up. Chicago State averages 65.5 points per game, 6.0 fewer points than the 71.5 LIU gives up to opponents. The teams square off for the third time this season. LIU won the last meeting 73-56 on Feb. 27. Jamal Fuller scored 24 to help lead LIU to the win, and Stephen Byard scored 17 points for Chicago State. TOP PERFORMERS: Fuller is scoring 16.6 points per game and averaging 5.2 rebounds for the Sharks. Malachi Davis is averaging 12.7 points and 3.6 rebounds over the last 10 games. Braelon Bush is averaging 8.4 points and 3.2 assists for the Cougars. CJ Ray is averaging 16.2 points over the last 10 games. LAST 10 GAMES: Sharks: 8-2, averaging 72.4 points, 30.8 rebounds, 13.8 assists, 8.3 steals and 5.1 blocks per game while shooting 49.9% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 64.1 points per game. Cougars: 5-5, averaging 66.5 points, 27.5 rebounds, 14.4 assists, 8.2 steals and 1.5 blocks per game while shooting 42.3% from the field. Their opponents have averaged 68.4 points. ------ The Associated Press created this story using technology provided by Data Skrive and data from Sportradar.
